Commercial Mar 26, 2026

Changi Airports International to help develop Phu Quoc as ‘airport destination’

Changi Airports International (CAI) announced it has signed a strategic cooperation agreement with Vietnam’s Sun Group to develop…

Changi's expansion into Vietnam signals Southeast Asian airports' shift from pure transportation hubs toward integrated resort destinations, creating new non-aeronautical revenue opportunities.

Military Mar 26, 2026

First Production KF-21 Boramae Rolled Out of Factory in South Korea

The first serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jet has rolled out of the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory in a major milestone for the program. The first of 40 serial production KF-21 Boramae fighter jets has rolled out of the Korea Aerospace Industries (KAI) factory, marking a major...

South Korea's indigenous fighter development demonstrates significant aerospace manufacturing capability while reducing dependency on foreign defense contractors for advanced combat aircraft.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Mar 26, 2026

NASA to test nuclear electric propulsion with 2028 mission to Mars

NASA’s new exploration strategy includes the rapid development of a nuclear-powered mission to Mars in 2028, leveraging hardware originally built for the lunar Gateway.

NASA's 2028 Mars nuclear mission repurposes existing lunar Gateway hardware, accelerating deep-space exploration timelines while reducing development costs through component reuse.

See SpaceNews →

How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.

Safety Management Systems succeed only when organizational culture genuinely rewards transparent reporting over blame, requiring leadership commitment beyond compliance checkbox exercises.

Airbus tests firefighting system linking drones, aircraft and ground crews

Airbus has demonstrated a concept in which diverse aerial and ground assets share a common data picture during…

Integrated drone-aircraft-ground coordination could accelerate wildfire response by enabling real-time data sharing and synchronized asset deployment across traditionally siloed emergency operations.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

A350-900 vs 777-300ER: Who Wins on Range?

For more than two decades, the Boeing 777-300ER has been one of the most recognizable long-haul aircraft in the world. It has carried millions of passengers across continents, linking global hubs like Dubai, New York, Hong Kong, and Los Angeles with remarkable reliability. Airlines have long...

The A350-900's superior fuel efficiency enables comparable range to the 777-300ER while carrying more passengers, reshaping long-haul economics for carriers evaluating fleet modernization.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before they cause incidents.

Commercial Mar 26, 2026

The Striking Differences Between The Boeing 777X & Airbus A350-1000

Find out how the Boeing 777X and Airbus A350-1000 Compete

The 777X's higher capacity and cargo volume appeal to legacy route operators, while the A350-1000's superior fuel efficiency and range advantages serve emerging long-haul markets.

Military Mar 26, 2026

Russia denies sending modified Shahed drones to Iran

Russia is reportedly close to completing shipments of modified Shahed-type drones to Iran, according to Western intelligence cited…

Western intelligence claims Russia's drone technology transfer to Iran signals deepening military cooperation that could reshape regional airspace security and drone proliferation dynamics.

FlyFocus unveils KURIER unmanned helicopter

FlyFocus, a Warsaw-based defence technology company specialising in unmanned aerial systems (UAS) and avionics, has

FlyFocus's KURIER represents Poland's expanding domestic UAS capabilities, potentially reducing European dependence on international suppliers for tactical unmanned helicopters.

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures demonstrate how terrain-constrained airports in busy Class B airspace balance operational efficiency with community relations.

Military Mar 26, 2026

Video Shows Super Hornet’s Near Miss After Apparent Iranian MANPADS Launch

A U.S. F/A-18 Super Hornet was involved in a near miss after being targeted by an apparent Iranian MANPADS system during a low altitude strafing run with the 20 mm M61 cannon. Videos emerged online on Mar. 26, 2026, show a U.S. Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet fighter jet targeted by an apparent Iranian...

Modern MANPADS effectiveness at low altitude during active combat operations demonstrates evolving threats to tactical aviation in contested airspace.

See The Aviationist →

Military Mar 26, 2026

The Top 5 Highest Paid US Air Force Pilot Ranks & Their Average 2026 Salaries

Air Force pilots are paid based on their rank and years of service, although even the highest ranks typically earn less than commercial pilots.

Despite premium flight hours and responsibility, top-tier Air Force pilot compensation remains structurally below commercial aviation earnings, highlighting persistent military-to-civilian talent migration pressures.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 26, 2026

DJI Avata 360 drone launch delayed in US

DJI’s 360° drone launch may already be underway globally, but US buyers could be stuck waiting. A newly updated banner on DJI’s official Amazon US storefront now points to March 30 at 8 a.m. EST as the release timing for its upcoming DJI Avata 360 drone, subtly overriding the March 26 worldwide...

DJI's US market entry for the Avata 360 is delayed to March 30, suggesting ongoing regulatory or supply chain constraints specific to American distribution channels.

See DroneDJ →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike fatalities persist despite pilot overconfidence in reaction time, making terrain awareness systems and operational discipline critical survival factors for rotorcraft operations.

Light Dynamix will host its second Discovery Event on Thursday 30th April 2026 at The

Light Dynamix's recurring Discovery Events position drone light shows as a maturing market segment attracting serious commercial interest beyond novelty applications.

Coast-To-Coast In 3 Hours: How The New Supersonic Bill Could Slash US Flight Times

Supersonic jets could revolutionary coast-to-coast travel in the US.

Regulatory approval pathways for supersonic aircraft are accelerating, enabling potential sub-3-hour transcontinental flights that could reshape domestic route economics and competitive positioning.

The Top 5 Largest US Navy Supercarriers By Active Aircraft Capacity

Discover the 5 largest US Navy supercarriers by aircraft capacity, led by USS Gerald R. Ford with unmatched airpower and next-gen technology.

The USS Gerald R. Ford's advanced aircraft handling systems enable 25% higher sortie rates than previous carriers, fundamentally reshaping naval aviation operational tempo.

See Simple Flying →

Industry Mar 26, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-flight systems offers immediate efficiency gains while technologies mature, creating a bridging strategy for sustainability amid projected traffic growth.

See MDPI →

Safety Mar 26, 2026

A Hot Start to Spring in the Southwest

Temperatures in several states climbed over 100 degrees Fahrenheit in March 2026, which in some places was the highest March temperature on record.

Record March heat in southwestern states may increase aircraft density altitude challenges and reduce runway performance during peak travel season.

Recovery logistics for crash survivor belongings depend on aircraft repositioning, creating extended hardship for passengers beyond the incident itself.

DJI just nuked drone prices across the board

Something strange is happening to DJI drone prices right now. And if you’ve ever even briefly considered buying a drone, this is one of those moments where ignoring it might come back to haunt you. Because this isn’t a typical seasonal sale or a small round of discounts tucked away on a product...

DJI's aggressive pricing strategy signals intensifying competition and market consolidation that could reshape consumer drone accessibility and competitive dynamics across the sector.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 26, 2026

Why The World’s Most-Produced Fighter Jet May Finally Disappear In 2026

Why the last combat-worthy MiG-21s likely disappeared in 2025, with only on-paper rusting hulks in service in 2026.

The MiG-21's retirement marks the end of a 70-year operational era, forcing air forces to accelerate modernization timelines and budgets for replacement aircraft.

La NASA ofrecerá cobertura en vivo de los eventos previos al lanzamiento, el...

NASA's comprehensive media coverage strategy for Artemis II demonstrates the agency's commitment to public engagement and transparency throughout the lunar mission's critical phases.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Ames Experts Available for Artemis II Flight Test Interviews

NASA’s Ames Research Center in Silicon Valley invites media to interview local subject matter experts on Friday, March 27 from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. ahead of the agency sending astronauts around the Moon for the first time in more than 50 years with the Artemis II flight test. NASA teams are gearing up...

NASA Ames is positioning Silicon Valley expertise as central to Artemis II's lunar mission success, signaling the critical role of regional research centers in deep-space exploration programs.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Sets Coverage for First Artemis Crewed Mission Around Moon

A variety of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for NASA’s Artemis II mission around the Moon will stream online. The agency is targeting no earlier than Wednesday, April 1, for the test flight during a two-hour window that opens at 6:24 p.m. EDT, with additional launch opportunities through...

NASA's April 2025 Artemis II lunar mission represents the operational validation of human-rated deep-space exploration infrastructure before the permanent lunar return attempted in Artemis III.

See NASA →

Space Force weighs launch alternatives as Vulcan faces potential months-long grounding

Officials explore shifting missions, extending satellites already in orbit

Space Force's contingency planning for Vulcan delays signals growing reliance on alternative launch providers, potentially accelerating competition in the national security launch market.

Delta is adding more Florida flying from LAX as its battle with United intensifies.

Delta's capacity expansion in underserved LAX-Florida routes signals shifting competitive dynamics and potential yield opportunities in high-demand leisure markets.

NASA Sets Coverage for Artemis II Moon Mission

Editor’s note: NASA will continuously update this Artemis II briefings and mission events page throughout prelaunch, launch, and mission activities. NASA will provide live coverage of prelaunch, launch, and mission events for the agency’s upcoming Artemis II crewed test flight around the Moon....

NASA's continuous live coverage strategy for Artemis II demonstrates the agency's commitment to public transparency during critical lunar mission phases, setting expectations for sustained media engagement throughout the multi-week mission timeline.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

Artemis Moon Tree Dedicated in Honor of Mary W. Jackson

On March 18, 2026, students, staff, and members of NASA’s Langley Research Center gathered at Mary W. Jackson Elementary School in Hampton to celebrate the dedication of a remarkable addition to the campus – an Artemis Moon Tree. Although formally dedicated on this day, the loblolly pine had...

NASA's symbolic planting of moon-exposed tree seeds at a school named after pioneering Black engineer Mary W. Jackson connects Artemis lunar missions to STEM inspiration for future aerospace professionals.

See NASA →

Regulatory Mar 25, 2026

US House passes bill to clear way for overland supersonic flights

The US House of Representatives passed legislation aimed at opening the skies to civil supersonic flight over land,…

Removing the 50-year overland supersonic ban could unlock a multi-billion dollar market for next-generation aircraft manufacturers while requiring FAA to establish new noise standards.

Eve's presidential demonstration signals strong government backing for eVTOL commercialization in Latin America, potentially accelerating certification timelines.

Aerospace Mar 25, 2026

NASA Names Jennifer Lyons Acting Launch Services Program Manager

NASA has selected Jennifer Lyons as acting program manager for the agency’s Launch Services Program (LSP) based at Kennedy Space Center in Florida. In this role, Lyons will lead NASA’s acquisition and management of domestic commercial launch services for science and robotic exploration missions...

NASA's appointment of Lyons signals continuity in managing relationships with commercial launch providers during a period of increased demand for science mission launches.
